propositions: Publication is gated only by a tag-push condition that does not verify the tagged SHA is an ancestor of reviewed main, allowing an arbitrary pushed tag to publish.

The three version values (pyproject.toml, server.json top-level, and packages[].version) are kept in lockstep only by a manual runbook, so a mismatch could publish disagreeing versions across PyPI, GitHub Release, and MCP channels.
The release should trigger via workflow_dispatch from main, enforcing github.ref == refs/heads/main, with version-lockstep and ancestry checks running before build.
Build and publish should occur directly via OIDC trusted publishing with no intermediate tag relay, and the v* tag plus GitHub Release created only after a successful publish as record-only output.
A release-time preflight must query the GitHub environments API and fail closed if the pypi environment lacks required reviewers or a main-only deployment-branch policy.
Guard logic (version-lockstep, ancestry, env-preflight parsing) should be extracted into unit-testable helpers verifying negative paths without a live publish.
